req.title: Manager creates org-announcement
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a org-announcement through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers manager-initiated org-announcement creation via UI with mandatory field validation and audit trail. I selected: (1) EP positive – valid manager submits all mandatory fields, expects 201 and entity id; (2) NEG negative – unauthenticated user attempts creation, expects 401; (3) BVA boundary – title field at max-length boundary (255 chars) submitted by manager, expects success; (4) DT decision-table – manager submits form with one mandatory field blank, expects 400 validation error; (5) STC state-transition – manager submits valid form, then verifies system transitioned to 'announcement created' state by checking audit record exists.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_announcement_all_valid_fields
actions: POST /org-announcements with all mandatory fields populated and valid manager auth token
expected: Response body contains a non-null entity id
trace: R-WEB-049: positive path – all mandatory fields supplied
Expected outcomes: org-announcement created; response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_manager_creates_announcement_all_valid_fields(manager_auth_headers, api_client, cleanup_announcement):
    payload = {
        "title": "Q1 Company Update",
        "body": "Important announcement for all staff.",
        "audience": "all"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/org-announcements", json=payload, headers=manager_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
    data = resp.json()
    entity_id = data.get("id")
    assert entity_id is not None, "Response must contain entity id"
    cleanup_announcement(entity_id)
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/org-announcements/{entity_id}", headers=manager_auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Created announcement must be retrievable"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_announcement
actions: POST /org-announcements with all mandatory fields but WITHOUT Authorization header

tests.name: test_create_announcement_title_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: Known max title length is 255 characters
actions: Construct title string of exactly 255 characters
POST /org-announcements with boundary-length title and valid manager auth token
expected: Response body contains a non-null entity id
trace: R-WEB-049: boundary – title at max permissible length
BVA applied to mandatory field 'title' upper boundary
tests.executable:
def test_create_announcement_title_at_max_length_boundary(manager_auth_headers, api_client, cleanup_announcement):
    MAX_TITLE_LEN = 255
    boundary_title = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LEN
    payload = {
        "title": boundary_title,
        "body": "Boundary value test body.",
        "audience": "all"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/org-announcements", json=payload, headers=manager_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
    data = resp.json()
    entity_id = data.get("id")
    assert entity_id is not None, "Response must contain entity id"
    cleanup_announcement(entity_id)
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/org-announcements/{entity_id}", headers=manager_auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("title") == boundary_title, "Stored title must equal 255-char input"

tests.name: test_create_announcement_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
actions: POST /org-announcements omitting mandatory field 'title' with valid manager auth token
Capture response status and validation errors
expected: Response body contains validation error referencing the missing field
trace: R-WEB-049: error pathway – 400 on validation error
DT: decision row where mandatory field 'title' is absent

tests.name: test_audit_record_written_after_successful_announcement_creation
actions: POST /org-announcements with all mandatory fields and valid manager auth token
GET /audit-log filtered by entity id and action 'create'
expected: HTTP status of creation is 200 or 201
Audit log contains exactly one record for the created entity with action 'create'
Audit record references the manager actor
trace: R-WEB-049: expected outcome – audit record written on success
STC: state transition from 'no announcement' to 'announcement created' verified via audit log
tests.executable:
def test_audit_record_written_after_successful_announcement_creation(manager_auth_headers, api_client, cleanup_announcement, manager_username):
    payload = {
        "title": "Audit Trail Test Announcement",
        "body": "Verifying audit record is written.",
        "audience": "all"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/org-announcements", json=payload, headers=manager_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status_code}"
    entity_id = resp.json().get("id")
    assert entity_id is not None
    cleanup_announcement(entity_id)
    audit_resp = api_client.get(
        "/audit-log",
        params={"entity_id": entity_id, "action": "create"},
        headers=manager_auth_headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200, f"Audit log query failed: {audit_resp.status_code}"
    records = audit_resp.json().get("results", [])
    assert len(records) >= 1, "At least one audit record must exist for the created announcement"
    assert any(r.get("actor") == manager_username for r in records), "Audit record must reference manager actor"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_create_announcement
preconditions: User is authenticated with role:employee (not manager)
actions: POST /org-announcements with all mandatory fields using employee auth token
trace: R-WEB-049: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
NEG: actor does not hold required role:manager
